reportingProfile.description: Reporting profile for the provider-backed node-count variation campaign.

baseline.purpose: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.
selectionCriteria: Keep the provider-backed baseline intentionally close to the historical application-level baseline so that infrastructure effects are not mixed with model, workload, or placement changes.
Use two RPC workers to preserve the validated LocalAI worker-mode topology before introducing resource, node-count, or placement variation cycles.
Use the smallest validated model family member to minimize footprint during the first provider-backed end-to-end cycle.
Use the controlled low-load workload to validate the full provider-backed pipeline before executing broader sweeps.
Use the explicit PL_COLOCATED placement profile for the first provider-backed baseline; later cycles can switch placement profiles while keeping model, workload, and infrastructure fixed.
lockRules: Do not mutate B1 after it has been used for a frozen provider-backed cycle.
Do not reuse B0 artifacts as evidence for B1.
Do not interpret B1 and B0 results as directly interchangeable without accounting for their different infrastructure profiles.
Use B1 as the provider-backed reference only after the declared proxmox-k3s cluster has passed validation.
Collect minimal observability evidence for B1 using metrics-server, kubectl snapshots and Locust artifacts before diagnosis and reporting.
baseline.notes: B1 intentionally inherits the application-level shape of B0 while switching to the INFRA_C1_1CP_2W_8C16G provider-backed infrastructure profile. This keeps the first provider-backed cycle focused on validating the provisioning-to-benchmark path rather than changing multiple dimensions at once.
baseline.scenarioProfileName: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.

payload.description: Comparative provider-backed campaign that varies the number of provider worker nodes while preserving per-node capacity, LocalAI worker count, model, workload and placement policy.

scope.primaryQuestion: How does the number of provider worker nodes affect LocalAI worker-mode latency, throughput and resource pressure when per-node capacity, model, workload, LocalAI worker count and placement policy remain fixed?

stateModel.profileStatus: Configuration-profile lifecycle state. This is the canonical meaning of the backward-compatible status field.
stateModel.configurationState: Whether the cycle configuration is active, locked, superseded, or deprecated.
stateModel.executionState: Latest known execution outcome for the cycle or campaign.
stateModel.status: Backward-compatible alias of profileStatus; execution outcomes must be read from executionState.

infrastructureSummarySemantics.description: Each campaign variant resolves its own infrastructure profile and provider binding; cycle-level reporting must aggregate variant infrastructure metadata.

payload.placementIntent: Spread active RPC workers across available worker nodes to reduce per-node worker concentration.
payload.researchQuestion: How does spreading the active RPC workers change latency and throughput when infrastructure and workload are fixed?

selectorPolicy.rationale: Stable node names are retained for compatibility with the current Kustomize overlays; label-based node-pool selection is the preferred direction for future generated clusters.

serverPlacement.placementNotes: The current server manifest is still node-name compatible; later topology profiles can replace this with a label-based selector.

mappingSemantics.activeWorkerNodeMapByWorkerCount: Application-level worker-count mapping currently defined for W2 and W4. The map is retained for deployment-profile compatibility; infrastructure-level mappings remain canonical for node-count driven provider-backed analyses.
mappingSemantics.activeWorkerNodeMapByInfrastructureWorke: Infrastructure-level placement mapping keyed by the number of Kubernetes worker nodes available in the generated cluster. This is the canonical map for provider-backed node-count and placement analyses across two-, three- and four-worker-node infrastructures.

compositionSelectionPolicy.selectionRationale: Provider-backed spread experiments should choose a topology by infrastructure worker-node count when that value is known; application worker-count mappings remain as compatibility fallbacks for deployment profiles that only declare LocalAI worker-count scenarios.

expectedBenefits: direct comparison against co-located worker placement
expectedRisks: server may remain close to only part of the worker set
network overhead can dominate for tightly coupled worker-mode requests

governance.comparisonPolicy: Placement comparisons must keep infrastructure, model, workload, worker count, protocol, and metric collection fixed unless a cycle profile explicitly declares otherwise.

payload.cycleName: C3 Node Count Variation Variant NC_2N
payload.description: Runtime-generated provider-backed variant cycle for NC_2N within the C3 campaign.

scenario.purpose: Node-count variation scenario with 2 provider worker nodes and a fixed four-worker LocalAI topology.
selectionCriteria: Keep model, workload, LocalAI worker count, per-node capacity and placement policy fixed so the campaign isolates infrastructure worker-node count.
Use four LocalAI RPC workers so adding provider worker nodes can alter actual worker distribution rather than leaving additional nodes idle.
Use the 8 vCPU / 16 GiB worker-node capacity identified as benchmark-ready by the preceding resource-capacity campaign.
lockRules: Do not change model, workload, LocalAI worker count or per-node capacity inside this scenario.
Do not interpret differences without checking placement, CPU, memory and unsupported-scenario evidence.
Use the scenario only within a declared node-count variation campaign or a documented derivative cycle.
scenario.notes: This scenario is designed for provider-backed node-count comparison. Extra provider worker nodes are intentionally paired with a fixed W4 application topology and a spread placement policy so distribution effects are observable.
scenario.scenarioLabel: 2 provider worker nodes / fixed W4 spread topology

applicationDeploymentResolution.interpretationRule: The static scenario profile declares the intended runtime-generated deployment profile instead of leaving the application deployment binding ambiguous.

scenario.scenarioProfileName: 2 provider worker nodes / fixed W4 spread topology

templateMetadata.generationPolicy: Only explicit placeholders delimited by double underscores may be substituted. Runtime generation must not perform global literal replacements such as C1 or B1.
